hero.tagline: Geology to coast. Cave to village to sea.

itineraryStops.name: Jeju Stone Park
itineraryStops.description: This is where the day begins to make sense. Stone Park gives the group a clear introduction to Jeju's volcanic identity before the route opens into coast, crater, cave, and village landscapes.
highlights: Stone figures, towers, and wide open grounds
Strong introduction to Jeju's stone culture
Clean photo zones with broad visual spacing
Indoor museum elements that deepen the geology story
timeUsed: Short orientation walk through the main stone zone
Photo and explanation time around the open stone fields
Museum section or closing interpretation before departure
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: Starting here gives the later coastal, crater, and lava-tube stops a clearer frame.

smartNotes.photo: Wide compositions work better here than tight close-ups.
smartNotes.facilities: Cleanest restroom and museum support in early route.
smartNotes.tip: This stop works best as a calm opener, not a rushed photo stop.

itineraryStops.name: Seopjikoji
itineraryStops.description: After Stone Park, the route opens outward. Seopjikoji brings in sea wind, ridge views, and open coastline before the day moves into lunch and the main Seongsan highlight.
highlights: Open ridge coastline and sea-facing paths
Seasonal flower fields depending on time of year
Lighthouse viewpoint and broad ocean framing
A lighter scenic stop with strong visual contrast to Stone Park
timeUsed: Arrival and short route choice at the entrance area
Main ridge walk with open coastal views
Photo stop and return with a relaxed pace
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: Seopjikoji opens the sea mood early, so Seongsan can land later as the stronger volcanic highlight.

smartNotes.photo: Wind and open sky make side-angle shots better than front-facing still poses.
smartNotes.tip: This stop is more exposed than the earlier stop, so wind and sun matter more here.

itineraryStops.name: Lunch
itineraryStops.description: Lunch sits in the middle of the route as a true reset, not just a break. It creates clean pacing before the day's main activity at Seongsan Ilchulbong.
highlights: Midday energy reset before the most active stop
Natural pause between coastal walking and crater activity
Better pacing for mixed-age groups
Cleaner rhythm for the second half of the day
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: Placing lunch here keeps the Seongsan stop more comfortable and less rushed.

smartNotes.photo: Focus on local dishes if interested in food photography.
smartNotes.tip: Midday pacing matters more than speed here; a clean lunch stop improves the whole second half.

itineraryStops.name: Seongsan Ilchulbong
itineraryStops.description: This is the main volcanic highlight of the day. Seongsan brings together crater scenery, coastal scale, and—depending on timing—the haenyeo show context that makes the stop feel even more distinctly Jeju.
highlights: UNESCO-listed volcanic crater landscape
Strong panoramic views around the base and upper routes
Iconic coastal geology and steep formation lines
Haenyeo show context adds local cultural meaning to the stop
timeUsed: Arrival, orientation, and entry area setup
Main walking segment, with route adjusted by group pace
Crater / base viewing and haenyeo show timing if available
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: After lunch, Seongsan becomes the natural main event of the day—active, scenic, and culturally distinct.

smartNotes.photo: Wider crater and coastline compositions usually work better than tight summit-only shots.
smartNotes.facilities: Good facilities at base, basic near summit.
smartNotes.tip: Group pace, weather, and crowd conditions can affect whether the stop leans more toward walking, viewing, or show timing.

itineraryStops.name: Ilchulland
itineraryStops.description: After Seongsan, Ilchulland softens the day's rhythm. The lava-tube-and-garden setting feels cooler, calmer, and more enclosed after the open crater and coast.
highlights: Lava-tube atmosphere within a more controlled garden setting
Visual contrast after the exposed Seongsan stop
Shaded pathways and softer pacing
Good transition from major highlight to late-day flow
timeUsed: Short garden finish and regroup before departure
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: Ilchulland works best here as a softer post-Seongsan transition, not as the day's main geological opener.

smartNotes.photo: Mixed indoor-outdoor lighting can create strong mood shots if exposure is kept balanced.
smartNotes.tip: This stop feels easier after Seongsan because the pace is more contained and shaded.

itineraryStops.name: Seongeup Folk Village
itineraryStops.description: Seongeup closes the day with lived culture rather than another major scenic push. After stone, coast, crater, and cave-garden textures, the village adds human scale and historical atmosphere.
highlights: Traditional thatched-roof homes and stone walls
A village setting that feels cultural rather than purely scenic
Local texture, heritage atmosphere, and slower walking rhythm
A strong closing contrast to the earlier natural landmarks
timeUsed: Walk through the main village paths and architectural points
Closing explanation and light browsing before departure
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: Ending here gives the day a more grounded finish after the stronger natural highlights.

smartNotes.photo: Narrow paths, rooflines, and wall textures usually work better than broad center-framed shots.
smartNotes.tip: This stop closes the route best when kept focused and unhurried.

routeShapeIntro.title: How this day moves
routeShapeIntro.subtitle: Stone to coast, lunch, then crater to village. 09:00 to 17:00.

items.label: Stone First, Before the Coast
items.detail: Stone Park frames Jeju first, before the route opens into wider sea views.

items.label: Lunch at Midday, Right on Time
items.detail: The route reaches lunch at noon, creating a clean reset before the main Seongsan stop.

items.label: Main Highlight After Lunch
items.detail: Seongsan sits right after lunch so the most active part of the day lands with better energy and a clearer rhythm.

items.label: Coast Before Crater
items.detail: Seopjikoji opens the sea mood first, then Seongsan carries the strongest volcanic highlight.

items.label: Garden After the Peak
items.detail: Ilchulland works as a softer lava-tube-and-garden transition after Seongsan.

items.label: Village at the End
items.detail: Seongeup closes the day with lived culture and local texture after the scenic highlights.

items.label: No Monday Departures
items.detail: This route does not operate on Mondays.

items.label: Weather & Wind
items.detail: Seopjikoji and Seongsan are the most weather-sensitive parts of the day.

items.label: Walking Balance
items.detail: Seongsan pacing can be adjusted depending on group energy, weather, and on-site conditions.

practicalAccordionItems.title: Pickup & drop-off
practicalAccordionItems.preview: Hotel lobby pickup in Jeju City area
content: Pickup from your hotel lobby between 08:30-09:00
Specific pickup time confirmed the evening before

practicalAccordionItems.title: Walking & terrain
content: Total walking: approximately 4-6 km across all stops
Stone Park: mostly flat with some uneven surfaces
Micheongul Cave: underground walkways, can be slippery
Seongsan: optional summit climb (~20 min, steep)

practicalAccordionItems.title: Weather & closure handling
practicalAccordionItems.preview: Runs in most conditions with adjustments
content: Heavy rain: cave stop prioritized, outdoor minimized
Strong wind: Seopjikoji may be shortened

seasonalVariations.name: Spring
seasonalVariations.description: Canola blooms paint the coast gold

bookingSupportSteps.title: Final Pickup Guide
bookingSupportSteps.detail: Exact pickup time and driver contact

staticQuestions.question: Is this good for first-time visitors to Jeju?
staticQuestions.answer: Yes. It combines cultural atmosphere, East Jeju coastal scenery, and iconic landmarks in one balanced route.

staticQuestions.question: Is the walking very difficult?
staticQuestions.answer: Not overall. The second half can feel more tiring, especially around Seopjikoji and Seongsan depending on weather and walking distance.

staticQuestions.question: Can the tour still run if it rains?
staticQuestions.answer: Yes. The route adjusts—cave stops are prioritized in heavy rain, and outdoor exposure is managed.

staticQuestions.question: Is it okay to join with parents?
staticQuestions.answer: Yes. The structure is generally comfortable, and exposed coastal sections can be shortened if needed.

staticQuestions.question: Can I join with children?
staticQuestions.answer: Yes. It is closer to scenic sightseeing than a high-activity family itinerary. Better for children 8+ who can handle moderate walking.

staticQuestions.question: Can the order change?
staticQuestions.answer: Yes. Coastal sections are sensitive to wind, traffic, and crowds; partial reordering can create a smoother flow—optimization, not a downgrade.

staticQuestions.question: Is lunch included?
staticQuestions.answer: That depends on the product setup; the route is designed with a meal break in the middle. Check your booking fare.

staticQuestions.question: Why start at Jeju Stone Park?
staticQuestions.answer: A calmer, more stable start. The route builds mood and scenery gradually instead of peaking immediately with open-sea views.

staticQuestions.question: Why is Seongeup placed next?
staticQuestions.answer: It adds traditional village texture after the stone landscape so the first half feels layered rather than one-note nature.

staticQuestions.question: Why is lunch placed there?
staticQuestions.answer: It resets energy before the more exposed and visually intense afternoon at Seopjikoji, Seongsan, and Hamdeok.

staticQuestions.question: Why is the later part structured this way?
staticQuestions.answer: Seopjikoji and Seongsan are signature highlights—grouping them focuses the scenic core of the day; Hamdeok releases intensity with a lighter finish.

guestReviews.author: Sarah M.
guestReviews.title: Perfectly paced day through East Jeju
guestReviews.text: The route made so much sense—starting at Stone Park gave us context for everything that came after. Our guide explained the geology in a way that made Seongsan and the lava tube much more meaningful. Lunch timing was perfect, and we had energy for the crater climb.

guestReviews.author: James K.
guestReviews.title: Best way to see East Jeju in one day
guestReviews.text: We've done several tours in Korea, and this one stood out for its thoughtful sequencing. The guide knew exactly when to slow down and when to move. The village at the end was a nice cultural finish after all the natural scenery.

guestReviews.author: Yuki T.
guestReviews.title: Great route, wish we had more time at Seongsan
guestReviews.text: The overall experience was excellent. Stone Park was surprisingly interesting, and the cave-garden stop was a nice break after the crater. Only reason for 4 stars is I would have liked 15 more minutes at Seongsan, but I understand the schedule constraints.

guestReviews.author: Michael R.
guestReviews.title: Exceeded expectations in every way
guestReviews.text: Came for the scenery, stayed for the storytelling. The guide connected each stop to the next in a way that made the whole day feel cohesive. The lunch spot was excellent and the small group size meant we could actually hear explanations at each stop.
